title: How Long Does Grey Hair Coverage Last?
body_text:
If you feel like your grey roots pop up almost immediately after your color appointment, you're not alone. At Studio One, we often hear customers ask why grey roots show after just two weeks. The truth is, even the best hair color has to compete with your natural hair growth cycle—and some types of gray are more stubborn than others.

The average person's hair grows about half an inch per month, or roughly one-eighth of an inch per week. So in just two weeks, you can already see a noticeable sliver of new growth. Because grey or white strands contrast starkly against most dyed colors, even the smallest regrowth can be very noticeable. This doesn't mean your color "didn't last"—it means your hair is doing what it naturally does: growing. For many Studio One guests, this means planning touch-ups every 3 to 5 weeks depending on hair growth rate and grey visibility.

When covering gray, experts recommend staying close to your natural root color to reduce contrast as hair grows in. Avoid colors more than two shades lighter or darker, as dramatic shifts make root regrowth more noticeable. Soft, dimensional color instead of solid tones can help mask grey regrowth more naturally.

If you're just starting to go gray or have only a few silver strands showing, highlights might be your secret weapon. Highlights add brightness and movement, drawing the eye away from isolated gray strands. They blend grays naturally, especially when placed strategically around your face and part line. They also require less frequent touch-ups compared to all-over color, making them a low-maintenance option.

If you've been coloring your hair for a while, you may notice some grays don't take color easily—or fade faster. These are called resistant grays, and they require a professional approach. Studio One uses professional-grade color formulas specifically designed to penetrate and fully saturate even the most stubborn grey strands, applying extended processing time, pre-softening methods, and custom blending.

Grey coverage typically lasts 3 to 8 weeks, depending on the type of dye used, your hair's growth rate, and your haircare routine. Frequent washing, sun exposure, and harsh shampoos can all shorten color longevity. If you're using permanent color and still seeing gray peeking through at the 2-week mark, it's likely due to new growth, not fading.

Studio One Hair Design specializes in custom color for every stage of your hair journey. Whether you're looking for soft blending with highlights or full gray coverage with maximum staying power, they help you choose a solution that fits your lifestyle and hair goals.
